description_long: This house consists of two adjacent single-story structures, each with a gable roof, connected by a shared open patio area with a pergola-style covering. The left structure features a light blue roof and a partially open side, revealing an interior with partitioned rooms and a visible entry door. The right structure has a dark blue roof, a solid exterior with horizontal siding, and a single window and door visible from the side. Both buildings are clad in white horizontal siding, and the patio area is paved with a stone or concrete surface. The open pergola roof over the patio is supported by wooden beams, providing partial shade. The overall design is simple and functional, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on utility.
description: A pair of single-story, gable-roofed houses with white siding, connected by a shared open patio with a pergola roof. The design is simple, with visible doors, windows, and a partially open interior.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open patio area between two gable-roofed houses, with visible interior partitions and a clear view of the pergola structure.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the white horizontal siding, windows, and doors of both houses, as well as the open pergola roof over the patio.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid backs of both houses, the gable roofs, and the patio area extending between them.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the entry doors, windows, and the open structure of the left house, with the pergola and patio clearly visible.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple entry doors with minimal overhang, no steps, and a shared open patio with a pergola structure
